steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content:
The blank falls after “Engravers' Copyright” and before “gave.”
Left of blank: An independent clause (“This changed in 1735 with the passage of the Engravers' Copyright”)
Right of blank: A verb phrase (“gave engravers control over the distribution and sale of all prints made from their designs”)

steps.title: Apply the rule for non-restrictive relative clauses
steps.content:
A non-restrictive relative clause introducing additional information is introduced by “which” and preceded by a comma.

Choice A (Act, which) correctly uses this structure. Choices B, C, and D violate this rule: B uses a semicolon (which joins independent clauses, not dependent ones), C starts a new sentence with “which” (incorrect), and D omits the relative pronoun entirely.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
